manifest.author: Alan Grainger
manifest.description: Embed Google Photos images using the new Picker API (Legacy Library API features removed)
manifest.name: Google Photos

releases.author: alangrainger
releases.description:
# Major update to work with Google's new Picker API

In March 2025, Google [removed the original photos API](https://developers.googleblog.com/en/google-photos-picker-api-launch-and-library-api-updates/) and replaced it with a **much worse and less capable** version called the Picker API. See discussion in issue #56 

Thanks to the amazing work of [@followcurly](https://github.com/followcurly), this plugin has been updated to work with the new Picker API.

- 🚨 **Important!** Please [read the Readme](https://github.com/alangrainger/obsidian-google-photos) for the changes.
- 🚨 **Important!** Follow the [setup guide for the Picker API](https://github.com/alangrainger/obsidian-google-photos/blob/main/docs/Setup-PickerAPI.md) to get the updated plugin to work.

[See the PR](https://github.com/alangrainger/obsidian-google-photos/pull/63) for all changes.

releases.author: alangrainger
releases.description:
# Improvements

- Replaced the Google OAuth method with one that proxies the requests transparently to an Obsidian protocol handler. This should resolve the issues for mobile devices and some Linux installations.

## 🚩 Important note for users prior to v1.6.0

The authentication method for this plugin was updated to make it more reliable, and to allow users with mobile devices to connect to Google Photos.

Because of this change you will need to update the **Redirect URI** that you have set up for the Photos API.

1. Go to [Google API Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials) and select the project you created when you set up the plugin.
2. Change the **Redirect URI** to be the new one specified in [Step 4 of the plugin documentation](https://github.com/alangrainger/obsidian-google-photos/blob/main/docs/Setup.md).
3. Allow 10 minutes for Google to make the change, and everything should work fine.

releases.author: alangrainger
releases.description:
# New features

You can now use a shorthand to insert a gallery of today's photos, or photos from the note date.

### Photos from the periodic note date

````
```photos
notedate
```
````

### Photos from today

````
```photos
today
```
````

releases.author: alangrainger
releases.description:
# New features

- Run the `Insert album` command to select from a list of your recent albums and insert it as a visual gallery.
- You can style or remove the album title with the `.google-photos-album-title` CSS selector.

# New features

Fantastic new features added thanks to https://github.com/Ebonsignori 

- Added the ability to display photos from a range of days (+/- today's date)
- Added the ability to use a frontmatter/YAML field for the date rather than note title

releases.author: alangrainger
releases.description:
# Initial release

This plugin let's you embed Google Photos images directly into Obsidian. When you select an image, it will save and embed a low-res thumbnail with a link back to the full-res image on Google Photos.

If your note title has a detectable date, you can have the plugin default to showing you only photos from that date. There is a toggle at the top to show all photos instead.
